UPC Barcode Quiz

1. How many digits are in a standard UPC-A barcode?



2. What is the purpose of guard patterns in a UPC barcode?



3. Which side uses odd-parity encoding in a UPC-A barcode?



4. How many digits are encoded on the left side?



5. How many digits are encoded on the right side?



6. What is the binary encoding for digit 0 on the left side?



7. What does the middle guard (01010) indicate?



8. What is the role of the check digit?



9. What is the binary encoding for digit 1 on the right side?



10. What are the start and end guards in UPC-A?



11. Is every 7-bit binary string a valid UPC digit encoding?


12. How many bits are used in total for encoding the digits (excluding guards)?



13. What kind of error should be raised if an invalid binary module is passed to your validator?



14. Is the UPC code 036000291452 valid based on its check digit?


Would you like to see feedback on your answers?
